headlines.title: U.S. begins retaliatory strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The U.S. has begun retaliatory strikes after Iran downed an Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, according to Central Command. "The mission is a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression,” CENTCOM wrote in a post on X.

headlines.title: Graham Platner Faces Key Test in Maine Primary
headlines.description: All eyes are on a key U.S. Senate race in Maine, where democratic senate hopeful Graham Platner, who has weathered a series of scandals, is likely to win a chance to unseat longtime Maine Senator Susan Collins in the general election. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: 2020 election denier under scrutiny from fair elections advocates for new role at DOJ
headlines.description: Kurt Olsen is now part of the Justice Department, working on a “grand conspiracy” investigation targeting the president’s foes.

headlines.title: Violent anti-immigrant protests flare up across Belfast after knife attack
headlines.description: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.

headlines.title: Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump’s term
headlines.description: The legislation, passed only with Republican votes after Democrats demanded reforms to the immigration agencies, now heads to President Trump for his signature.

headlines.title: Jury reaches guilty verdict in Karmelo Anthony murder trial
headlines.description: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of the 2025 murder of fellow high school student Austin Metcalf at a district-wide meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb. Anthony’s legal team argued he acted in self-defense after he’d sat in the bleachers under the tent of rival high school Memorial and was confronted by members of its track team and told to leave.

headlines.title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew
headlines.description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

headlines.title: Soccer Fever Kicks in Across US Ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup
headlines.description: In just one week, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to North America for the first time in more than 30 years and the United States men’s national team is looking to stretch the limits of homefield advantage. It will be the final World Cup appearance for Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo and possibly Lionel Messi of Argentina as well. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Paramount accuses Netflix of ‘scorched-earth’ campaign against Warner Bros. deal
headlines.description: Paramount accused Netflix of launching a “scorched-earth campaign to try and poison regulators and other stakeholders” against its purchase of Warner Bros.

headlines.title: FDA allows popular sunscreen ingredient long used in Europe and Asia
headlines.description: The Food and Drug Administration on Tuesday expanded its list of allowed sunscreen ingredients to include the chemical compound bemotrizinol.

headlines.title: Inside Google’s AI training for teachers
headlines.description: Google flew 70 teachers and administrators to its California headquarters to learn about its AI products — and how to persuade skeptical colleagues to use them.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.
